op.name: polyadd
op.notes: Add two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.
op.summary: Find the sum of two polynomials.

blocks.text: Find the sum of two polynomials.

blocks.name: note
blocks.body:
This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.
blocks.raw:
.. note::
   This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.

blocks.text:
Returns the polynomial resulting from the sum of two input polynomials.
Each input must be either a poly1d object or a 1D sequence of polynomial
coefficients, from highest to lowest degree.

desc_blocks.text:
The sum of the inputs. If either input is a poly1d object, then the
output is also a poly1d object. Otherwise, it is a 1D array of
polynomial coefficients from highest to lowest degree.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.polyadd([1, 2], [9, 5, 4])
array([9, 6, 6])

>>> p1 = np.poly1d([1, 2])
>>> p2 = np.poly1d([9, 5, 4])
>>> print(p1)
1 x + 2
>>> print(p2)
   2
9 x + 5 x + 4
>>> print(np.polyadd(p1, p2))
   2
9 x + 6 x + 6

blocks.text: poly, polyadd, polyder, polydiv, polyfit, polyint, polysub, polyval
